title: Keep the control loop deterministic
description: Run control at a fixed rate with bounded execution time, moving all heavy or blocking work out of the real-time path.
body: Use when implementing or reviewing any periodic control loop driving actuators. Do not perform allocation, logging to disk, network I/O, or unbounded computation inside the loop, and avoid sharing locks with non-real-time threads. Actions: run the loop at a fixed period on a real-time-priority thread, preallocate buffers, do heavy work (planning, perception) in separate threads communicating via lock-free or bounded queues, and measure worst-case execution time against the period. Validation signals include a jitter histogram showing the loop meets its deadline under load and no missed cycles. Risk boundary: a control loop that misses its deadline produces unstable or unsafe actuator motion; treat blocking work in the real-time path as a critical timing defect and escalate before running on hardware.
